title: Get your graduate degree in Canada - Canada.ca
content:
## Get your graduate degree in Canada
Get your master’s or doctoral degree at one of Canada’s world-class learning institutions

## Choose Canada for your graduate studies

### Faster processing for doctoral students
Get your application processed in 2 weeks if you’re a doctoral student.

### No cap on certain graduate students
As of January 1, 2026, you won’t need a provincial or territorial attestation letter.

### Apply with your family
Your family can apply to come to Canada with you.

### Stay and work in Canada
Stay and work in Canada with a work permit.

## Canada’s world-class learning institutions
We’re 6th on the global list of Highly Cited Researcher award winners.
Our education system is in the top 10 of the Global Employability rankings.
( Times Higher Education, 2021 )
Four Canadian universities rank in the top 100 worldwide.
( topuniversities.com, 2025 )

## Study in Canada as a graduate student
How to pursue your graduate studies in Canada.
Apply for a study permit

### Life as a graduate student
Learn about study costs, housing, healthcare and more.
Life as a graduate student

### Work in Canada after you graduate
Find out what type of work permit you need to work in Canada after you graduate

## Priye from Nigeria: Why I chose to conduct PhD research in Canada
My name is Priye Iworima and I am a PhD candidate at the University of British Columbia, where I study research on diabetes. Growing up as a little child in Nigeria, I always knew I wanted to be in the sciences.
Why did I choose Canada?
I had friends that were in Canada.
They talked about the science that went on in Canada, how innovative it, it was, and the expertise that Canada had.
						... and the rest is history.
Diabetes is a global disease that is affecting hundreds of millions of people.
And these cells that we're making, we hope one day will help ease the burden of living with diabetes.
Some advice I would give to students that want to come to Canada to study [regenerative medicine] are really do the homework.
[...] Push yourself outside your comfort zone [...]
[...] Because you really never know who you're talking to and how they can impact your life in the future or how you can impact their life in the future.
Regenerative medicine right now is a really exciting time.
And you have the opportunity to collaborate with a lot of people as well.
So that really pushes the science and the research further. We are really not an island here.
So It made sense for me to come to a place like here and I really loved that decision.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
